## NOTES FOR DEVELOPMENT
================================================================================

### High-Priority Feature Gaps (categories with limited Neuman Brain coverage):
1. Habit Tracking - No current implementation
2. Journaling / Daily Logs - No current implementation
3. Focus/Pomodoro - No current implementation
4. Bookmark/Link Management - Only Link Library exists
5. Whiteboard/Diagramming - No current implementation
6. Personal CRM - No current implementation
7. Automation/Workflow - Basic automations exist
8. Meeting Notes/Transcription - No current implementation
9. Finance/Budgeting - No current implementation
10. Spaced Repetition/Flashcards - No current implementation
11. Voice/Audio Notes - No current implementation
12. Quick Capture/Inbox - Limited implementation
13. Subscription Management - No current implementation
14. Book/Reading Tracking - No current implementation
15. Meal Planning - No current implementation
16. Home Maintenance - No current implementation
17. Fitness/Workout Tracking - No current implementation
18. Travel Planning - No current implementation
19. Data Backup/Export - Basic export exists
20. Weekly/Daily Review - No structured implementation

### Key Differentiators to Pursue:
- Local-first / privacy-first (like Obsidian, Anytype)
- Open source when possible
- E2E encryption for sensitive data
- Offline-capable
- No vendor lock-in (standard formats)
- AI-enhanced but privacy-respecting
- Cross-platform (web, desktop, mobile)
- Self-hostable option
- Keyboard-first design (Superhuman philosophy)
- 100ms response time target
- Block-based content (Notion philosophy)
- Plugin/extension ecosystem (Obsidian philosophy)

### UX/UI Priorities to Implement:
1. **Speed**: Target 100ms response time (Superhuman standard)
2. **Keyboard Navigation**: Cmd+K palette, shortcuts for everything
3. **Zero-Friction Capture**: Quick add from anywhere
4. **Progressive Disclosure**: Simple by default, power when needed
5. **Micro-Animations**: Tactile feedback (Things 3 standard)
6. **Dark Mode First**: With OLED optimization
7. **Command Palette**: Universal search/action (Linear/Raycast)
8. **Slash Commands**: Quick formatting (Notion)
9. **Native Feel**: Platform-specific optimizations (Craft)
10. **Real-Time Collaboration**: Multi-cursor support (Figma)

### Trends in 2025:
- AI integration in every category
- Local-first gaining momentum
- Open source alternatives thriving
- Privacy consciousness increasing
- Consolidation (all-in-one tools)
- Cross-device sync expectations
- Mobile-first for many categories
- Voice-first interfaces emerging
- Memory/recall AI assistants
- Opinionated software over flexible
- Body doubling / social accountability
- Digital minimalism tools growing
- Spaced repetition mainstream
- E-ink devices for deep work
- Personal AI agents

### Competitive Positioning Matrix:

| Competitor | Strength | Weakness | Neuman Brain Opportunity |
|------------|----------|----------|--------------------------|
| Notion | Flexibility, blocks | Slow, cloud-dependent | Local-first, speed |
| Obsidian | Local, plugins | Steep learning curve | Easier onboarding |
| Todoist | Natural language | Single-purpose | All-in-one integration |
| Things 3 | Apple design | Apple-only | Cross-platform |
| Linear | Developer UX | Team-focused | Personal productivity |
| Superhuman | Speed | $30/mo, email only | Affordable all-in-one |
| ClickUp | Features | Overwhelming UI | Simplicity |
| Roam | Networked thought | $15/mo, learning curve | Free + intuitive |
| **Fizzy** | Vibrant UI, fast, OSS | Kanban-only, O'Saasy license | Full productivity suite, MIT license |

### Integration Opportunities (APIs/Protocols):
- **CalDAV/CardDAV**: Calendar/contacts interop
- **WebDAV**: File sync standard
- **ActivityPub**: Federated social (Mastodon-style)
- **Matrix Protocol**: Decentralized messaging
- **ICAL**: Calendar export/import
- **Markdown**: Universal note format
- **OPML**: Outline/RSS exchange
- **JSON-LD**: Structured data
- **OAuth 2.0**: Service connections
- **Webhooks**: Event-driven automation

### Potential API Integrations to Consider:
1. **GitHub** - Issue/PR tracking for developers
2. **Linear** - Two-way sync for dev tasks
3. **Todoist** - Import/migration path
4. **Google Calendar** - Calendar sync
5. **Obsidian** - Vault compatibility
6. **Readwise** - Highlight import
7. **Strava** - Fitness data
8. **Spotify** - Focus music integration
9. **OpenAI/Anthropic** - Local AI with API fallback
10. **HomeAssistant** - Smart home triggers

### Feature Priority Framework (Impact vs Effort):

**High Impact, Low Effort (Do First):**
- Command palette (Cmd+K)
- Keyboard shortcuts system
- Quick capture global hotkey
- Dark mode improvements
- Search improvements

**High Impact, High Effort (Plan Carefully):**
- Voice notes with transcription
- Habit tracking
- Journaling/daily review
- Pomodoro/focus timer
- Whiteboard/diagramming

**Low Impact, Low Effort (Quick Wins):**
- Subscription tracking (simple list)
- Book tracking (simple list)
- Travel packing lists
- Weekly review templates

**Low Impact, High Effort (Avoid/Defer):**
- Full meal planning with recipes
- Complete fitness tracking
- Home maintenance scheduling
- Full personal CRM

### Platform Vision Phases:

**Phase 1 - Foundation (Current):**
- Tasks, Notes, Calendar, Time Tracking
- Dashboard widgets
- Basic automations

**Phase 2 - Capture Everything:**
- Quick capture (global hotkey)
- Voice notes + transcription
- Web clipper
- Email integration

**Phase 3 - Review & Reflect:**
- Daily/weekly review
- Journaling
- Habit tracking
- Goal setting (OKRs)

**Phase 4 - Focus & Deep Work:**
- Pomodoro timer
- Focus mode
- Distraction blocking
- Session tracking

**Phase 5 - Visualize & Connect:**
- Graph view (knowledge connections)
- Whiteboard/canvas
- Mind mapping
- Relationship visualization

**Phase 6 - Life Management:**
- Finance tracking
- Health/fitness integration
- Travel planning
- Home management

### Success Metrics to Track:
- Time to first action (< 2 seconds)
- Interaction latency (< 100ms)
- Daily active usage
- Feature adoption rates
- User retention (30/60/90 day)
- Data export usage (trust indicator)
- Community plugin contributions

================================================================================
## SOURCES & REFERENCES (Updated December 2025)
================================================================================

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me analyze and provide my strategic recommendation:

---

## Strategic Analysis: What Should Come Next?

Based on my analysis of the codebase state, task files, and your three thoughts, here's my recommendation:

### Current State Summary
- **Codebase health:** 9/10 (TypeScript strict, 220 tests passing, build succeeds)
- **Features shipped:** 147+ (~45% of roadmap)
- **Build size issue:** `providerRouter-CKKSKVtW.js` is 1,570KB (needs attention)
- **Active task files:** 3 (audit-phase2, docs-system, alternatives research)

---

## My Recommendation: **Option 3 First, Then Option 1**

### Why This Order?

1. **Technical debt now = compound savings later.** The codebase audit (phase 2) is 80% documented with specific actionable items. Cleaning now prevents accumulation.

2. **Build size warning is a signal.** That 1.5MB chunk (`providerRouter`) will hurt users. This aligns with your Option 3 goal.

3. **Polish before expand.** Your Option 1 (enhance existing features) will be faster/cleaner after Option 3 cleanup.

---

## Concrete Next Step: Complete Codebase Audit Phase 2

The `tasks/codebase-audit-phase2-10-10.md` file has a clear checklist that directly addresses your goals:

| Phase 2 Item | Your Goal Alignment |
|--------------|---------------------|
| Split Settings.tsx (1,700→~300 lines) | Option 3: leaner, cleaner |
| Split CardDetailPanel.tsx (1,750→~400 lines) | Option 3: maintainability |
| Bundle optimization (160KB→<120KB entry) | Option 3: performance |
| CSP headers | Option 3: security |
| Store subscription cleanup | Option 3: efficiency |

**Estimated effort:** 6-9 hours across sessions

---

## After Audit Phase 2: Quick Wins from Option 1

Once cleanup is done, these "enhance existing features" items would have high impact:

1. **Synapse polish** (your last commits show this is active)
2. **Global command palette enhancements** (recently shipped)
3. **Dashboard widget refinements**

---

## What About the Docs System (Option 2)?

The `docs-system-plan.md` explicitly requires **Phase 0: Deep Research** (8-12 hours) before any implementation. This is a larger undertaking best tackled after the quick wins above.

---

## Proposed Sequence

```
Week 1: Codebase Audit Phase 2A (Settings.tsx split)
Week 2: Codebase Audit Phase 2B (CardDetailPanel.tsx split)  
Week 3: Codebase Audit Phase 2C (Bundle + Security)
Week 4+: Option 1 - Polish existing features
Later:  Option 2 - Docs system (requires research first)
```

---

## Do You Want Me To...

1. **Start the Codebase Audit Phase 2A now?** (Settings.tsx split - 2-3 hours)
2. **Investigate the bundle size issue first?** (the 1.5MB chunk is urgent)
3. **Review all three task files with `/consider:pareto`** to identify the 20% effort for 80% impact?
4. **Something else?** Your thoughts 1, 2, 3 are all valid paths - I'm recommending order, not discarding any.

message.content: Yes, I agree - all of it. So let's proceed in the order you determine to be the best longterm foundational supporting option.

content.text:
Now I understand the problem clearly. The issue is that `providerRouter.ts` statically imports all 9 AI provider SDKs at the module level:
- `openai` (used by OpenAI, OpenRouter, xAI, DeepSeek)
- `@anthropic-ai/sdk` (Anthropic)
- `@mistralai/mistralai` (Mistral)
- `@google/generative-ai` (Gemini)
- `groq-sdk` (Groq)
- `@huggingface/inference` (HuggingFace)

This creates a 1.5MB chunk that loads even if the user never uses AI features.

**Solution:** Dynamic imports for AI providers - only load the SDK when that specific provider is actually used.

Let me check how the providers are currently structured:

   113→

input.content:
# Task: AI Provider Lazy Loading (Bundle Optimization)

**Status:** Completed
**Date:** 2024-12-05
**Impact:** 99.2% reduction in providerRouter chunk size (1,570KB → 12.70KB)

## Problem

The `providerRouter-CKKSKVtW.js` chunk was 1,570KB gzipped because it statically imported all 9 AI provider SDKs at module level:
- `openai` (OpenAI, OpenRouter, xAI, DeepSeek)
- `@anthropic-ai/sdk` (Anthropic)
- `@mistralai/mistralai` (Mistral)
- `@google/generative-ai` (Gemini)
- `groq-sdk` (Groq)
- `@huggingface/inference` (HuggingFace)

This meant users downloaded all SDKs even if they only used one provider (or none).

## Solution

Implemented dynamic imports via a new `providerLoader.ts` module:

### Architecture Changes

1. **New `providerLoader.ts`**
   - Static `PROVIDER_METADATA` for UI display (no SDK required)
   - Static `PROVIDER_MODELS` subset for initial display
   - `loadProvider(id)` - dynamically imports SDK on first use
   - Provider caching to prevent duplicate loads
   - API key management integrated with lazy loading

2. **Updated `providerRouter.ts`**
   - Changed `getProvider()` to async (loads SDK on demand)
   - Added `getProviderSync()` for UI-only access
   - Added `getAllProviderMetadata()` for sync metadata access
   - API keys stored in router, applied when provider loads

3. **Updated consumers**
   - `AITerminal.tsx` - uses `configuredProviderCount` state for sync UI checks
   - `ProviderSettings.tsx` - uses `PROVIDER_METADATA` for display
   - `ModelSelector.tsx` - async loading with fallback to static models
   - `UsageTracker.tsx` - uses `PROVIDER_METADATA` and `getProviderModels()`
   - `linkCategorizer.ts` - async provider checks
   - `aiCommandService.ts` - async provider checks

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/services/ai/providerLoader.ts` | NEW - lazy loading infrastructure |
| `src/services/ai/providerRouter.ts` | Refactored to use providerLoader |
| `src/components/AITerminal.tsx` | Async provider access |
| `src/components/ProviderSettings.tsx` | Uses static metadata |
| `src/components/ModelSelector.tsx` | Async model loading |
| `src/components/UsageTracker.tsx` | Uses static metadata |
| `src/services/linkCategorizer.ts` | Async provider checks |
| `src/services/phantomShell/aiCommandService.ts` | Async provider checks |

## Bundle Impact

**Before:**
- `providerRouter-CKKSKVtW.js`: 1,570KB

**After:**
- `providerRouter-6atKMWxB.js`: 12.70KB (metadata only)
- Provider SDKs load on-demand:
  - `groqProvider`: 29.69KB
  - `anthropicProvider`: 69.83KB
  - `huggingFaceProvider`: 316KB
  - `mistralProvider`: 1,005KB (largest SDK - Mistral)

## Verification

- [x] TypeScript type check passes
- [x] All 220 tests pass
- [x] Production build succeeds
- [x] Bundle size reduced 99.2%

## Notes

- The Mistral SDK is still large (1MB), but now only loads when user configures/uses Mistral
- Most users will likely use OpenRouter or Groq (smaller SDKs with free tiers)
- Future optimization: Consider replacing Mistral SDK with fetch-based implementation

1→# Codebase Audit - Phase 2: Path to 10/10 Health
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→Complete the remaining audit items to achieve 10/10 codebase health. Phase 1 brought us from 8/10 to 9/10 by fixing TypeScript strictness and adding security tests. This phase addresses structural improvements.
     5→
     6→## Current State: 9/10
     7→- ✅ TypeScript strictness (no `any` leaks)
     8→- ✅ Security tests for critical services (72 tests)
     9→- ✅ React hook dependencies fixed
    10→- ✅ Store subscriptions using selector pattern
    11→- ✅ Comprehensive UX patterns (loading, empty states)
    12→
    13→## Target State: 10/10
    14→
    15→---
    16→
    17→## Priority 1: Large File Splits
    18→
    19→### 1.1 Split Settings.tsx (1,700 → ~5 × 300 lines)
    20→**Impact:** High (maintainability, load time)
    21→**Complexity:** Medium-High
    22→
    23→**Current structure:**
    24→- Delegates to 10+ sub-components already
    25→- ~800 lines of inline sections remain
    26→
    27→**Sections to extract:**
    28→| Section | Est. Lines | Notes |
    29→|---------|------------|-------|
    30→| AccountSettings | ~50 | User profile, avatar |
    31→| SiteWideSettings | ~100 | Background, display prefs |
    32→| TaskManagementSettings | ~60 | Auto-shift, WIP limits |
    33→| AITerminalSettings | ~100 | API config, model selection |
    34→| QuickActionsSettings | ~60 | Export/Import brain buttons |
    35→| AutoSaveSettings | ~300 | Directory picker, config |
    36→| BackupHistorySettings | ~60 | History list, restore |
    37→| CalendarSettings | ~80 | Import/export, notifications |
    38→| AdvancedSettings | ~200 | Storage info, browser info |
    39→
    40→**Challenge:** Shared state across sections
    41→- `preferences` object used by multiple sections
    42→- `backupHistory` state
    43→- `autoSaveDirectory` state
    44→- Various loading/error states

# Codebase Audit - Phase 2: Path to 10/10 Health

## Overview
Complete the remaining audit items to achieve 10/10 codebase health. Phase 1 brought us from 8/10 to 9/10 by fixing TypeScript strictness and adding security tests. This phase addresses structural improvements.

## Current State: 9/10
- ✅ TypeScript strictness (no `any` leaks)
- ✅ Security tests for critical services (72 tests)
- ✅ React hook dependencies fixed
- ✅ Store subscriptions using selector pattern
- ✅ Comprehensive UX patterns (loading, empty states)

## Target State: 10/10

---

## Priority 1: Large File Splits

### 1.1 Split Settings.tsx (1,700 → ~5 × 300 lines)
**Impact:** High (maintainability, load time)
**Complexity:** Medium-High

**Current structure:**
- Delegates to 10+ sub-components already
- ~800 lines of inline sections remain

**Sections to extract:**
| Section | Est. Lines | Notes |
|---------|------------|-------|
| AccountSettings | ~50 | User profile, avatar |
| SiteWideSettings | ~100 | Background, display prefs |
| TaskManagementSettings | ~60 | Auto-shift, WIP limits |
| AITerminalSettings | ~100 | API config, model selection |
| QuickActionsSettings | ~60 | Export/Import brain buttons |
| AutoSaveSettings | ~300 | Directory picker, config |
| BackupHistorySettings | ~60 | History list, restore |
| CalendarSettings | ~80 | Import/export, notifications |
| AdvancedSettings | ~200 | Storage info, browser info |

**Challenge:** Shared state across sections
- `preferences` object used by multiple sections
- `backupHistory` state
- `autoSaveDirectory` state
- Various loading/error states

**Approach options:**
1. Lift state to Settings index, pass as props (prop drilling)
2. Create SettingsContext for shared state
3. Move state to Zustand store (if persistence needed)

---

### 1.2 Split CardDetailPanel.tsx (1,750 → ~4-5 × 350 lines)
**Impact:** High (critical Kanban component)
**Complexity:** High

**Current structure:**
- ~30 useState hooks for local editing state
- 6 tabs with complex content
- Multiple store connections (Kanban, TimeTracking, Settings, Template)

**Components to extract:**
| Component | Est. Lines | Notes |
|-----------|------------|-------|
| TaskMetadataSection | ~400 | Title, description, dates, priority, assignees |
| TaskDependencySection | ~150 | Dependency picker, shift confirmation |
| SubtasksTab | ~180 | Subtask list with CRUD |
| ChecklistTab | ~90 | Checklist items |
| TimeTrackingTab | ~110 | Timer, entries list |
| AttachmentsTab | ~65 | File attachments |

**Challenge:** Tightly coupled local state
- Editing state (title, description, dates, etc.) shared across sections
- Handlers depend on local state
- Task prop needed everywhere

**Approach options:**
1. Create `useTaskEditor` custom hook to encapsulate all editing state/handlers
2. Use React Context for task editing state
3. Keep state in parent, pass handlers as props (verbose but explicit)

---

## Priority 2: Bundle Optimization

### 2.1 Entry Chunk Size (160KB → <120KB)
**Impact:** Medium (initial load time)
**Complexity:** Low-Medium

**Analysis needed:**
- Run `npx vite-bundle-visualizer` to identify what's in entry chunk
- Check if any heavy dependencies are accidentally bundled in entry

**Potential optimizations:**
- More aggressive lazy loading boundaries
- Review manual chunks configuration in vite.config.ts
- Ensure tree-shaking is working for large libraries

---

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`src/services/ai/providerLoader.ts`** (NEW - 391 lines)
     - Core infrastructure for lazy loading AI provider SDKs
     - Contains static `PROVIDER_METADATA` and `PROVIDER_MODELS` for sync UI access
     - `loadProvider(id)` function dynamically imports SDKs on first use
     - Key code pattern:
     ```typescript
     export async function loadProvider(providerId: string): Promise<AIProvider> {
       if (loadedProviders.has(providerId)) {
         return loadedProviders.get(providerId)!;
       }
       // Dynamic import based on provider ID
       switch (providerId) {
         case 'openrouter': {
           const { openRouterProvider } = await import('./openRouterProvider');
           provider = openRouterProvider;
           break;
         }
         // ... other providers
       }
       loadedProviders.set(providerId, provider);
       return provider;
     }
     ```

   - **`src/services/ai/providerRouter.ts`** (refactored)
     - Changed from static imports to using providerLoader
     - `getProvider()` now async, added `getProviderSync()` for UI-only access
     - Added `apiKeys` Map for storing keys before providers load
     - Key change: removed all static provider imports at top

     - Added `isLoading` state and loading indicator
     - Fallback to static model list on error

   - **`src/components/ProviderSettings.tsx`** (26 line changes)
     - Uses `PROVIDER_METADATA` for display instead of loading providers
     - Async `handleTestApiKey` and `saveProviderKey` functions

   - **`src/components/UsageTracker.tsx`** (12 line changes)

     - Uses `router.getProviderModels()` for sync model access

   - **`src/services/linkCategorizer.ts`** (12 line changes)
     - `isAIConfigured()` and `getConfiguredProviderNames()` now async
     - `categorizeLinks()` awaits `router.getConfiguredProviders()`

   - **`src/services/phantomShell/aiCommandService.ts`** (2 line changes)
     - Added `await` to `router.getConfiguredProviders()` call

   - **`tasks/ai-provider-lazy-loading.md`** (created, then archived)
     - Documents the 99.2% bundle reduction achievement

   - **`src/pages/Settings.tsx`** (being analyzed for Phase 2A)
     - 1,748 lines, needs to be split into ~5 × 300 line components
     - Has ~30 useState hooks with shared state
     - Already has some extracted components in `src/widgets/Settings/`

## Priority 1: Large File Splits

### 1.1 Split Settings.tsx (1,700 → ~5 × 300 lines)
**Impact:** High (maintainability, load time)
**Complexity:** Medium-High

**Current structure:**
- Delegates to 10+ sub-components already
- ~800 lines of inline sections remain

**Sections to extract:**
| Section | Est. Lines | Notes |
|---------|------------|-------|
| AccountSettings | ~50 | User profile, avatar |
| SiteWideSettings | ~100 | Background, display prefs |
| TaskManagementSettings | ~60 | Auto-shift, WIP limits |
| AITerminalSettings | ~100 | API config, model selection |
| QuickActionsSettings | ~60 | Export/Import brain buttons |
| AutoSaveSettings | ~300 | Directory picker, config |
| BackupHistorySettings | ~60 | History list, restore |
| CalendarSettings | ~80 | Import/export, notifications |
| AdvancedSettings | ~200 | Storage info, browser info |

**Challenge:** Shared state across sections
- `preferences` object used by multiple sections
- `backupHistory` state
- `autoSaveDirectory` state
- Various loading/error states

**Approach options:**
1. Lift state to Settings index, pass as props (prop drilling)
2. Create SettingsContext for shared state
3. Move state to Zustand store (if persistence needed)

---

### 1.2 Split CardDetailPanel.tsx (1,750 → ~4-5 × 350 lines)
**Impact:** High (critical Kanban component)
**Complexity:** High

**Current structure:**
- ~30 useState hooks for local editing state
- 6 tabs with complex content
- Multiple store connections (Kanban, TimeTracking, Settings, Template)

**Components to extract:**
| Component | Est. Lines | Notes |
|-----------|------------|-------|
| TaskMetadataSection | ~400 | Title, description, dates, priority, assignees |
| TaskDependencySection | ~150 | Dependency picker, shift confirmation |
| SubtasksTab | ~180 | Subtask list with CRUD |
| ChecklistTab | ~90 | Checklist items |
| TimeTrackingTab | ~110 | Timer, entries list |
| AttachmentsTab | ~65 | File attachments |

**Challenge:** Tightly coupled local state
- Editing state (title, description, dates, etc.) shared across sections
- Handlers depend on local state
- Task prop needed everywhere

**Approach options:**
1. Create `useTaskEditor` custom hook to encapsulate all editing state/handlers
2. Use React Context for task editing state
3. Keep state in parent, pass handlers as props (verbose but explicit)

---

## Priority 2: Bundle Optimization

### 2.1 Entry Chunk Size (160KB → <120KB)
**Impact:** Medium (initial load time)
**Complexity:** Low-Medium

**Analysis needed:**
- Run `npx vite-bundle-visualizer` to identify what's in entry chunk
- Check if any heavy dependencies are accidentally bundled in entry

**Potential optimizations:**
- More aggressive lazy loading boundaries
- Review manual chunks configuration in vite.config.ts
- Ensure tree-shaking is working for large libraries

---

## Priority 3: Security Hardening

### 3.1 CSP Headers for Production
**Impact:** Medium (security)
**Complexity:** Low

**Implementation:**
- Add Content-Security-Policy headers via Cloudflare
- Or add `<meta>` tag in index.html for basic CSP

**Recommended policy:**
```
default-src 'self';
script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval';
style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';
img-src 'self' data: blob: https:;
connect-src 'self' https://api.openai.com https://api.anthropic.com;
```

---

## Priority 4: Minor Cleanup

### 4.1 Store Subscription Cleanup
**Impact:** Low (minor re-render prevention)
**Complexity:** Low

**Files to update:**
- `src/pages/Settings.tsx:114` - `useCalendarStore()` full destructure
- `src/pages/Settings.tsx:121` - `useTerminalStore()` full destructure
- `src/pages/LinkLibrary.tsx:55` - `useLinkLibraryStore()` full destructure

**Change pattern:**
```tsx
// Before
const { events, importEvents } = useCalendarStore();

// After
const events = useCalendarStore((s) => s.events);
const importEvents = useCalendarStore((s) => s.importEvents);
```

---

## Optional: Additional File Splits

### Notes.tsx (1,308 lines)
- Approaching recommended limits but functional
- Could split into NotesListPanel, NoteEditorPanel, NotesSidebar
- Lower priority than Settings/CardDetailPanel

### Sidebar.tsx (759 lines)
- Largest layout component
- Could extract: SidebarNavigation, SidebarFavorites, SidebarRecent
- Lower priority

---

## Checklist

### Phase 2A: Settings.tsx Split
- [ ] Design state management approach (props vs context vs store)
- [ ] Extract AccountSettings section
- [ ] Extract SiteWideSettings section
- [ ] Extract TaskManagementSettings section
- [ ] Extract AITerminalSettings section
- [ ] Extract QuickActionsSettings section
- [ ] Extract AutoSaveSettings section
- [ ] Extract BackupHistorySettings section
- [ ] Extract CalendarSettings section
- [ ] Extract AdvancedSettings section
- [ ] Create Settings/index.tsx orchestrator
- [ ] Verify all settings still work
- [ ] Run tests

### Phase 2B: CardDetailPanel.tsx Split
- [ ] Design useTaskEditor hook or context
- [ ] Extract TaskMetadataSection
- [ ] Extract TaskDependencySection
- [ ] Extract SubtasksTab
- [ ] Extract ChecklistTab
- [ ] Extract TimeTrackingTab
- [ ] Extract AttachmentsTab
- [ ] Update CardDetailPanel to compose components
- [ ] Verify all Kanban functionality works
- [ ] Run tests

### Phase 2C: Bundle & Security
- [ ] Analyze entry chunk with bundle visualizer
- [ ] Implement optimizations if obvious wins found
- [ ] Add CSP headers to production deployment
- [ ] Fix remaining store subscription patterns

### Final Verification
- [ ] `npm run type-check` passes
- [ ] `npm run test` passes (220+ tests)
- [ ] `npm run build` succeeds
- [ ] Bundle size improved or maintained
- [ ] Manual smoke test of Settings and Kanban

---

## Success Criteria
- Settings.tsx < 300 lines (orchestrator only)
- CardDetailPanel.tsx < 400 lines (orchestrator only)
- Entry chunk < 120KB gzipped (stretch goal)
- All tests pass
- No regressions in functionality

## Estimated Effort
- Phase 2A (Settings split): 2-3 hours
- Phase 2B (CardDetailPanel split): 3-4 hours
- Phase 2C (Bundle & Security): 1-2 hours
- Total: 6-9 hours across multiple sessions

## Risk Assessment
| Change | Risk | Mitigation |
|--------|------|------------|
| Settings split | Medium | Test each setting after extraction |
| CardDetailPanel split | High | Critical path - thorough Kanban testing |
| Bundle optimization | Low | Performance only, no functionality change |
| CSP headers | Low | Test in staging first |
